straps_falling.symptom: Straps keep falling off your shoulders
causes: Band is too loose — riding up in back pulls straps off shoulders
Straps set too wide for your frame
Cup is too big, causing excess fabric that pulls straps
solutions: Try a smaller band size (and go up a cup to keep volume)
Look for bras with close-set or convertible straps
Try a racerback style or use a strap converter clip

band_rides_up.symptom: Band rides up in the back
causes: Band is too loose — should be level or slightly lower in back
Cup is too small, pulling everything upward
solutions: Size down in the band (size up in cup to keep volume)
Make sure you're on the loosest hook when buying new
Check that cups fully contain all tissue after swoop and scoop

cups_gaping.symptom: Cups are gaping or have empty space
causes: Cup shape doesn't match your breast shape (not necessarily wrong size)
Cup is too projected for a shallow shape
Bra has shifted — tissue may have migrated out of cup
solutions: Try a different cup shape before changing size
Shallow shapes: try molded cups, demi styles, or bralettes
Make sure to swoop and scoop all tissue into cups

quadboob.symptom: Breast tissue spilling over the top of cups
causes: Cup is too small
Cup is too shallow for projected tissue
Cup shape cuts across tissue instead of containing it
solutions: Try going up one or two cup sizes
If projected: try unlined seamed cups for more depth
Try a different style — full cup may work better than demi

wire_pain.symptom: Underwire is painful, digs in, or pokes
causes: Wire is sitting on breast tissue instead of around it
Wire is too narrow for your root width
Cup is too small, pushing wire onto tissue
solutions: Size up in cup so wire can sit in your inframammary fold
Wide roots: look for brands with wider wires (Elomi, Goddess)
Narrow roots: look for narrow wires (Ewa Michalak, Comexim)

cup_too_shallow.symptom: Cups feel too flat or shallow
causes: Molded/foam cups force a round shape that doesn't match projected tissue
Cup may be correct volume but wrong shape
solutions: Try unlined, seamed cups which allow more projection
Balconettes and plunge styles often offer more depth
Polish brands (Ewa Michalak, Comexim) are known for projected cuts

causes: Band is genuinely too small
Cup is too small — tissue pushes against band making it feel tighter
You may be adjusting to a correctly-fitted snug band after wearing loose ones
solutions: Try going up a cup size first — this often fixes perceived band tightness
If cups fit well, try sister size up (e.g., 32F -> 34E)
New bras should fasten on the loosest hook — they stretch over time

band_too_loose.symptom: Band feels loose, rides up, or doesn't provide support
causes: Band is too large
Band has stretched out from wear
You're fastening on the tightest hook on a new bra
solutions: Size down in band (go up a cup to keep volume)
The band should be snug — you should fit two fingers under it
Replace bras that can't be tightened further
